claim: Third MCC measurement. Balanced 20-gene sweep at doubled scale (0.1) and doubled bio-time (1.0 s) via parallel sweep script + calibration. Result: MCC=0.229 (TP=1, FP=0, TN=10, FN=9). Same MCC as the v0 4-gene panel; confirms that scaling up scale or t_end in tractable ranges does NOT improve recall. The detector only catches central-glycolysis disruption (pgi) regardless of config. Recall floor is fundamental to what 0.5 - 1 s at <=10%% scale can reveal in the metabolite pools.
caveats: Tried scale=0.5 (the knockout-test canonical value) but timed out at 9 minutes - runtime prohibitive for the interactive budget.

MCC does not improve from v0 (0.333 at n=4) or v1 (0.160 at n=40) to v2 (0.229 at n=20); the apparent differences are sample-size noise, not signal. The underlying predictor catches exactly one gene class (central glycolysis via F6P depletion) regardless of the scale / t_end knobs in the tried range.

The path to MCC > 0.59 requires changes beyond detector tuning: (1) simulate LONGER (minutes of bio-time, needs a faster simulator), OR (2) add non-metabolic signals (protein-complex assembly failure, translation rate, tRNA charging), OR (3) use a fundamentally different detection strategy (integrate trajectory divergence over time, not a threshold on peak deviation).
